theme: Ignite a few charges to route a chain reaction
title: Quarry Ignition: Routing a Chain Detonation Through Shielded Charges
seed_problem: closure-routed threshold cascade over a directed radius-reach graph with a limited ignition budget
mutation: composite: goal is a graded ignition-set optimization (not decision) over a directed, per-node-radius reach graph; output is restricted to an 'ignitable' subset while high-value 'shielded' charges require a distinct-source detonation threshold, so feasibility itself encodes the routing constraint
strategies: trivial: pick the M ignitable charges with the largest own value, ignoring reach entirely -- exactly the checker's baseline construction -> ratio 0.1

greedy: single continuous marginal-gain pass -- each round ignite whichever remaining charge adds the most SIMULATED total value right now. Legitimate and generally decent (never wastes a pick on an already-saturated cluster), but myopic across components: a shielded target that needs TWO distinct feeder chains lit together never looks attractive round-by-round, since lighting only one feeder unlocks nothing, so its small standalone marginal gain keeps losing to another cluster/decoy's larger visible payoff and the pair is never committed together

strong: decompose the reach graph into weakly-connected components, compute each component's best achievable value for every ignition budget k (via bounded local search per component, since components are small and isolated by construction), then solve a 0/1 group-knapsack over components to allocate the global M-budget -- this is the only tier that can 'see' a shielded target's value jump only when BOTH of its distinct feeder chains are lit together, which a single running marginal-gain pass (biased toward smooth, always-visible cluster gains) systematically starves of budget
invalid: prints ignition index 0, which is out of range [1,N] -- infeasible on every case -> 0
